# TapTrao

> Trade compliance SaaS for commodity traders sourcing from Africa. Check every document, duty, and regulation for any African commodity corridor — before you commit.

## What TapTrao does

- **Compliance checks**: Enter commodity + origin + destination to see every required document, duty rate, and regulatory trigger for that corridor
- **Regulatory screening**: EUDR (deforestation), CBAM (carbon), SPS (phytosanitary), IUU (fishing), CITES (endangered species), Kimberley Process, Lacey Act, REACH
- **LC cross-validation**: Upload Letter of Credit terms and trade documents for automated cross-checking against UCP 600 rules
- **Supplier document collection**: Generate zero-login upload links for suppliers — they see exactly which documents to provide, no account needed
- **Readiness scoring**: Get a compliance score with specific fix recommendations before shipping
- **TwinLog audit trail**: Every compliance event recorded with SHA-256 integrity hashing for audit-proof evidence
- **Regulatory alerts**: Monitor corridors for regulatory changes (EUDR deadlines, duty rate updates, SPS rule changes)
- **Demurrage calculator**: Estimate port demurrage costs before shipment

## Coverage

- **191 commodities**: cocoa, coffee, cashew nuts, shea butter, sesame seeds, timber, rubber, cotton, gold, diamonds, vanilla, tea, tobacco, palm oil, and 177 more
- **54 African origin countries**: Ghana, Cote d'Ivoire, Nigeria, Kenya, Tanzania, Ethiopia, Uganda, Cameroon, Senegal, Madagascar, Mozambique, DRC, Rwanda, Burkina Faso, and 40 more
- **7 destination markets**: EU, UK, USA, Canada, Switzerland, Turkiye, UAE

## Pricing (USD)

- **TapTrao Shield — Single shipment**: $189
- **3-Pack**: $499 ($166 per shipment)
- **5-Pack**: $799 ($160 per shipment)
- **LC-only check**: $49.99 (standalone, without trade credit)
- **LC check is included free** with every Shield pack

No subscription. No contract. No ERP integration required. Preview any corridor for free on the homepage.

## Who it's for

- Small and mid-size commodity traders importing from Africa who lack dedicated compliance departments
- Traders who need to understand regulatory requirements before committing to a purchase
- Importers coordinating with overseas suppliers for missing export/import documents
- Companies using letters of credit who need document cross-checking and an audit trail
- Anyone preparing for EUDR (30 Dec 2025) or CBAM (1 Jan 2026) enforcement

## Key regulatory dates

- **EUDR**: Entry into application 30 December 2025 for large/medium operators; 30 June 2026 for micro/small enterprises
- **CBAM**: Fully operational from 1 January 2026
- **UK Border Target Operating Model**: Full SPS checks on all food/plant imports
